verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein (p24/TMED family) with a large N-terminal luminal GOLD domain and a short cytosolic C-terminal tail. Its dominant compartment is the ER/ERGIC/Golgi, where it cycles via COPI/COPII vesicles as a cargo receptor. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document a genuine plasma membrane pool: (1) GeneCards/RefSeq/Wikipedia explicitly annotate localisation to 'the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae'; (2) TMED2/TMED10 are required for plasma membrane lipid nanodomain formation and subcompartmentalization; (3) TMED10 regulates cell-surface expression of the TAM receptor Axl; (4) TMED10 inhibition reduces PD-1 cell-surface stability. At the PM, the luminal GOLD domain faces the extracellular leaflet, making it accessible to binders. This is dual_localization: the protein has its own TM anchor and a documented minority PM pool alongside the dominant endomembrane home. No pMHC-only story; not a secreted protein; not inner-leaflet anchored.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The PM pool is a minor fraction relative to the dominant ER/Golgi compartment; no direct surface-biotinylation or flow-cytometry quantification of the PM-resident fraction was retrieved, making the magnitude of surface accessibility uncertain.
